chunk_summary: This section outlines discharge criteria for children recovering from MAM, emphasizing sustained anthropometric recovery, dual metrics (MUAC ≥125 mm and WHZ/WLZ ≥ -2), and community-based follow-up to prevent relapse.
learning_objectives: Understand the discharge criteria for children recovering from MAM, including anthropometric thresholds.
Learn the importance of dual metrics (MUAC and WHZ/WLZ) in assessing recovery and discharge readiness.
Recognize the need for community-based follow-up and caregiver education to prevent relapse after discharge.
key_facts: WHO 2023 guidelines discourage using percentage or absolute weight gain as discharge criteria.
Recovery is defined as MUAC ≥125 mm and WHZ/WLZ ≥ -2, maintained for at least two consecutive visits.
No bilateral pitting oedema should be present for at least two consecutive visits before discharge.
If only one metric is available, it should be documented, and high-risk children should be monitored closely.
Discharge planning must include a feasible feeding plan, caregiver education, and scheduled follow-up.
reference_quote: The 2023 WHO wasting guideline discourages using percentage weight gain or absolute weight gain as outpatient exit criteria and instead requires sustained anthropometric recovery.
document_key_topics: Caregiver education and feeding plan feasibility
